proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.1pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is heavily UP_TRI (53/54 = 98%) — if regime flips to Bear, this Bear-gated UP_TRI promotion would activate atop an already UP_TRI-concentrated book; monitor signal-type concentration on regime transition
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +41.1pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +41.1pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, day 7), so the cohort is dormant and the 100% Choppy concentration on 54 open positions is not compounded. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-23) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.8pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio is heavily skewed to UP_TRI (53/54) with only 1 BULL_PROXY position; if Bear re-emerges, a TAKE_SMALL boost may still meaningfully shift exposure mix
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +30.8pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +30.8pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, day 7), so the cohort is dormant and the 100% Choppy concentration trigger on 54 open positions is not compounded; a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY promotion may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 06-23) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Choppy regime 100%; UP_TRI 98.1%; UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank 22.22% (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Choppy regime 100%; UP_TRI 98.1%; UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank 22.22%; recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI and sector=Bank until (a) Bank cell share drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits, and (b) regime diversifies off pure Choppy. Do not add to Pharma/FMCG/Chem either — they are one entry away from breaching 0.20.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip unwinds the entire 54-position long book simultaneously with no statistical edge support (cohort is Candidate tier). Bank-led drawdown would compound the 22% sector cluster; correlated long-only exposure means no offsetting positions to dampen mark-to-market shock.

evidence: regime_concentration triggered: Choppy at 1.0 vs 0.80 threshold (54/54 positions)
signal_type concentration: UP_TRI at 53/54 = 98.1% vs 0.85 threshold
cell breach: UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank at 12/54 = 22.22% vs 0.20 threshold
additional cell clusters: UP_TRI×Choppy×Pharma 16.67%, FMCG 12.96%, Chem 12.96% (just below cell threshold but compounding sector clustering)
matched cohort regime=Choppy__signal_type=UP_TRI is Candidate tier (n=238) — unvalidated edge
book fully directional long: UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 54/54 = 100%
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI and sector=Bank until (a) Bank cell share drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits, and (b) regime diversifies off pure Choppy. Do not add to Pharma/FMCG/Chem either — they are one entry away from breaching 0.20.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip unwinds the entire 54-position long book simultaneously with no statistical edge support (cohort is Candidate tier). Bank-led drawdown would compound the 22% sector cluster; correlated long-only exposure means no offsetting positions to dampen mark-to-market shock.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (observed 1.0, above 0.80 threshold) AND UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank cell breaches the 0.20 cell threshold at 22.22% (12 of 54 positions). UP_TRI also dominates signal mix at 53/54 = 98.1% (above 0.85 threshold) with the book fully directional long. Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=238) — unvalidated edge across all concentration cells.
